description_long: This is a preserved crab specimen with a broad, oval-shaped carapace that is slightly domed and displays a muted grayish-green coloration on its dorsal side. The crab features two prominent, upward-curving claws (chelae) that are pale at the tips and darker at the base, with visible segmentation and joint articulation. Its walking legs are slender, jointed, and covered in a mix of light tan and brown hues, radiating outward from the body. The ventral side is lighter, almost creamy white, and the crab's eyes are set on short stalks protruding from the front of the carapace. The specimen is displayed on a flat, white textured surface with a color calibration cube for scale. The overall appearance suggests a hard, chitinous exoskeleton typical of marine crabs.
description: A preserved crab with a broad, gray-green carapace, prominent upward-curving claws, and slender, jointed legs. The surface is hard and chitinous, with eyes on short stalks and a pale underside.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's face, both large claws raised, and eyes on stalks clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the arch of the claws and the segmentation of the legs, with the pale underside partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, rounded back of the carapace and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the curvature of the claws and the lateral profile of the body and legs.
